records.title: Call 112 for life-threatening emergencies
records.description: The European emergency number 112 connects callers in the Netherlands to ambulance, police or fire services when immediate help is needed because life, safety or serious injury is at risk.
records.ai_summary: Call 112 when every second counts and state what happened, the exact location and which service appears necessary. Follow the operator's instructions while help is dispatched.

records.title: Use the 112NL emergency app
records.description: The government 112NL app can connect users to the emergency control room and support translated chat when hearing, speech or language makes an ordinary emergency call difficult.

official_source.title: Kan ik in noodgevallen de 112NL app gebruiken?

records.ai_summary: Install and configure the official app before an emergency, including language and accessibility needs. In danger, use it to call 112; the control room can start translated chat when speaking is difficult.

records.title: Accessible ways to contact 112
records.description: People who are deaf, hard of hearing or speech impaired can use supported text, relay and app options to contact the 112 control room, with some services requiring advance registration.

official_source.title: Hoe kan ik alarmnummer 112 bereiken als ik doof of slechthorend ben?

records.ai_summary: Set up the preferred accessible emergency method in advance and understand its hours or registration requirements. The 112NL app supplements direct calling and can enable chat with the control room.

attributes.audience: People with hearing or speech accessibility needs

records.title: Choose GP, out-of-hours GP or 112
records.description: Routine problems go to the registered GP, urgent problems outside normal hours go to the regional out-of-hours GP service, and immediate life-threatening danger requires 112.
records.ai_summary: Use the ordinary GP during opening hours, call the regional urgent GP service for problems that cannot wait until the practice reopens, and call 112 for immediate danger to life.

records.title: How the acute-care network is organised
records.description: Dutch acute care connects ambulance services, out-of-hours GP centres, hospital emergency departments, acute obstetrics and crisis mental healthcare to deliver care within minutes or hours.
records.ai_summary: Acute-care services cooperate regionally, and the correct entry point depends on severity and time of day. Triage can redirect the patient to the appropriate GP, ambulance, crisis or hospital service.

records.title: Regional urgent GP services
records.description: Out-of-hours GP centres provide urgent primary care during evenings, nights and weekends and use clinical triage to decide between advice, consultation, home visit or escalation.
records.ai_summary: Call before travelling to an out-of-hours GP centre so a triage professional can assess urgency and arrange the right response. The service is for care that cannot wait for the regular practice.

records.title: Immediate help for suicidal thoughts
records.description: A person thinking about suicide should tell someone and seek direct help through a trusted person, GP, current clinician or 113, while immediate danger to life requires 112.

official_source.title: Ik denk aan zelfmoord, waar vind ik hulp?

records.ai_summary: Do not stay alone with immediate suicide risk. Call 112 for current danger, or contact 113, the GP, urgent GP service or existing mental-health clinician for direct support and safety planning.

records.title: Help someone in an acute mental-health crisis
records.description: For an acute psychological crisis, contact the person's treating team or the GP and urgent GP service; call 112 when the person poses an immediate danger to self or others.

official_source.title: Iemand die ik goed ken heeft een psychische crisis. Wat moet ik doen?

records.ai_summary: Stay calm, reduce immediate hazards and contact the existing clinician or GP service for urgent assessment. If anyone's life is in danger or violence is imminent, call 112 immediately.

records.title: Use an AED during cardiac arrest
records.description: Anyone in the Netherlands may use an automated external defibrillator during resuscitation, following the device prompts while emergency services are called and chest compressions continue.

official_source.title: Wat is een automatische externe defibrillator (AED) en wie mag een AED bedienen?

records.ai_summary: Call 112, start resuscitation and send someone for an AED if available. Attach the pads and follow the spoken instructions; the device analyses the rhythm and only advises a shock when appropriate.

records.title: Find urgent dental care outside opening hours
records.description: Dental practices must arrange continuity for urgent pain, bleeding or trauma outside regular hours through a duty service or emergency dental practice and tell patients how to reach it.
records.ai_summary: Check the regular dentist's voicemail and website for the regional emergency service, call before attending and explain pain, bleeding, swelling or trauma. Call 112 instead for immediate danger to life.
